meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first professional project.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' barrier, you must move beyond simple tutorials. Use Antigravity to architect a production-ready feature and Claude Code to automate the Git versioning process. By documenting your commit history as a narrative of problem-solving, you transform raw code into a compelling professional portfolio for recruiters.
content.intro:
The screen stares back, blinking with a cursor that feels heavier than it should. You’ve finished the courses, watched the videos, and memorized the syntax, yet your GitHub profile remains a graveyard of 'Hello World' repositories. The industry doesn't care about your certificates; it cares about your ability to ship, debug, and iterate in a messy, real-world environment.

But the tide is turning. By leveraging AI-augmented workflows, you can stop just writing code and start orchestrating it. We are moving beyond manual terminal commands into a new era where Antigravity handles the architectural heavy lifting and Claude Code acts as your senior engineering mentor. It is time to turn your terminal into a bridge between 'student' and 'software engineer.'

steps.title: Define Your Minimum Viable Product
steps.description: Stop aiming for a 'clone.' Build one specific, high-utility feature that solves a real problem. This step focuses on scope definition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Developer. Help me define a scope for a single-feature SaaS tool that solves a specific problem in [insert niche]. Provide a directory structure and a tech stack recommendation that is modern but beginner-friendly.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Logic
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the backbone of your application, ensuring clean separation of concerns from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the core business logic for [feature]. Include type-safe interfaces, error handling, and a modular structure that allows for future testing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create the core logic based on the Antigravity architecture provided.'

steps.title: Automating Version Control Narratives
steps.description: Your Git history is your resume. Use AI to ensure every commit message describes the 'why' and 'what' of the change.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current code changes and write a professional, concise Git commit message following the Conventional Commits specification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'feat: implement [feature] with robust error handling'

steps.title: Refactoring for Performance
steps.description: Junior code works; senior code is maintainable. Use AI to identify bottlenecks and suggest cleaner implementations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my code for performance bottlenecks and readability improvements. Suggest 3 refactors that adhere to SOLID principles.

steps.title: Documenting the 'Why' for Recruiters
steps.description: Transform your README into a case study. Explain the technical challenges you faced and how you solved them using AI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for my project. Include a 'Problem Solved' section, a 'Technical Decisions' section, and instructions for local installation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a professional README.md file based on the current project structure.'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Lessons Learned' section in your README; recruiters value reflection more than raw lines of code.
Use Git branches for every feature; it shows you understand professional team workflows and CI/CD pipelines.
Treat your AI assistant as a junior developer: verify every suggestion, ask for the 'why' behind the code, and never blindly copy-paste.
Tag your releases in GitHub; it demonstrates an understanding of software versioning and deployment cycles.
Keep your commits atomic; one logical change per commit makes your history easier to audit during a technical interview.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, deploy it to Vercel or Netlify, share a link on LinkedIn with a summary of the technical challenges you overcame, and start your next project using a different tech stack to show versatility.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to turn code into a career. Don't let the 'no portfolio' excuse hold you back for another day. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
